LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LOIMATAFIA & NICOLE CARLOS-VALENTINO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND LEAVE TO DEFEND. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. ON 4/10/08, AFTER SOLOMON WITHDREW, PLAINTIFF SENT A LETTER TO DEFENDANTS WARNING THEM TO ANSWER. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANTS WERE RE-SERVED WITH THE COPIES OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IN AUGUST 2008 AND DID NOTHING. WHILE SOLOMON MAY HAVE CONTRIBUTED TO THE ERROR, HE WAS NOT SOLELY AT FAULT. (SEE LANG VS. HOCHMAN (2000) 77 CAL.APP. 4TH 1225.) DEFENDANTS SHOULD HAVE TAKEN SOME AFFIRMATIVE STEPS TO DEFEND THEMSELVES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Leave To Defend
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LOIMATAFIA & NICOLE CARLOS-VALENTINO Motion to Set Aside Default and Leave to Defend. Appearances: Martin N. Lettunich, Esq. Attorney at Law, (408) 395-3100 appearing for Defendants and moving party; Philip Avila, Esq. of Borton Petrini LLP (209) 576-1701 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. On 4/10/08, after Solomon withdrew, plaintiff sent a letter to defendants warning them to answer. Additionally, defendants were re-served with the copies of the summons and complaint in August 2008 and did nothing. While Solomon may have contributed to the error, he was not solely at fault. (See Lang vs. Hochman (2000) 77 Cal.App. 4th 1225.) Defendants should have taken some affirmative steps to defend themselves. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
